proposed.name: contract-identity
proposed.claim: A node is contract-grade iff its type is directive and its rules or invariants array is non-empty - contract is the machine-checkable grade of a directive, not a type. The c-prefix ID convention is a naming reflection of this structural property, not the definition of contract identity. Code that operates on contracts must detect them via Belief.contract?/1 and never by ID prefix matching.

rules.then: The function returns true iff the node has non-empty rules or non-empty invariants

rules.given: A new contract-grade directive is being assigned an ID
rules.then: The ID should use the c-prefix convention (c001, c002, ...) to flag the contract nature to human readers

rules.scenario: Contract structure cannot be retrofitted to descriptive types
rules.given: A primitive, compound, or inference is being edited to add rules
rules.then: The write is rejected - contract grade exists only on directives; mint a directive that deps on the descriptive node instead

invariants: Belief.contract?/1 is the canonical check for contract identity
Contract-grade directives may use retired status (per the status lifecycle)
c-prefix is a convention, not a structural guarantee - always check rules/invariants, not the ID

reasoning: cb-schema-v2: contract-grade re-attaches to the directive type (c031's biconditional preserved verbatim); the retired-status invariant rewords per the c029 successor's widening.
